response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise and distributed systems background directly applicable to routing pipelines, but critical concern is that primary requirement—modern C++ development—is severely outdated (last relevant work ~2015, with decade of TypeScript/web focus since). While candidate demonstrates rapid learning across tech stacks and the role explicitly emphasizes willingness to expand expertise, a mid-senior individual contributor position in C++ data pipelines typically requires more recent language depth to avoid significant ramp-up time.
strengths: Solid geospatial/GIS domain expertise from multiple projects (Green Map, GISCollective) directly relevant to routing tile generation and map data transformation

Proven distributed systems knowledge from Dunnhumby's microservices infrastructure (millions of requests/second) and experience with Kubernetes deployments, applicable to pipeline reliability and scale
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, quality assurance focus) and demonstrated ability to learn and work across diverse technical stacks
concerns: C++ experience is severely outdated (last hands-on work at National Instruments 2014–2015); primary recent work is TypeScript/Node.js, creating substantial ramp-up risk for a modern C++ role
No Rust experience despite it being listed as acceptable alternative; lacks specific routing/navigation engine background beyond general GIS and geospatial mapping
No visible data pipeline/ETL experience or background transforming raw geospatial data into optimized tile formats or handling real-time data freshness at scale
